question: A Spring Boot application sets only `camunda.client.auth.username` and `camunda.client.auth.password` and leaves `camunda.client.auth.method` unset. Which authentication method does the starter choose?

options.text: `basic`, because the method is auto-detected from the presence of a username and password.
`oidc`, because the auth client only supports OAuth flows when configured via Spring Boot.
`none`, because `method` is `null` and no explicit method was selected.
Startup fails — `camunda.client.auth.method` is mandatory and has no auto-detection.

a.text: The properties reference says of `camunda.client.auth.method`: "If not set, it is detected based on the presence of a username, password, client ID, and client secret." Configuring only username and password resolves to `basic`.

b.text: OIDC is auto-selected when `client-id` and `client-secret` are present, not when only `username`/`password` are configured.

c.text: `none` is one of the enum values, but it is not the auto-detected outcome when basic credentials are supplied.

d.text: The property is not mandatory; the starter explicitly auto-detects an appropriate method when it is left unset.

explanation: `camunda.client.auth.method` has an enum of `none|basic|oidc` and a `null` default. The docs state the method is auto-detected from configured credentials, so username+password → `basic`, and client-id+client-secret → `oidc`.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
